title: VerifierCommentary
description: A third-party (human reviewer or LLM probe) read on a VerificationReview, per design/verification-sketch-01.md V7 + PFS10-VC1..VC4. Distinct from VerificationReview itself: VerificationReview is the verifier's verdict on a record; VerifierCommentary is a reading of that verdict. Routes through the proposal queue the same way — the author walks and decides whether to act on it (extend the check function, accept the dissent, file away as noted). target_review uses cross-file $ref to verification_review.json per PFS10-X2 (intra-namespace, same-role, larger-evolving-record pattern).

commenter_id.description: Stable identifier for the commenter (human handle or LLM-probe identifier). PFS10-VC2.

commented_at_τ_a.description: Author-time τ_a at which the commentary was recorded. PFS10-VC2.

assessment.description: Closed enum matching Python's ASSESSMENT_* constants. endorses = verdict is well-grounded; qualifies = verdict stands but with clarification; dissents = commenter disagrees with the verdict with specific counter-argument; noted = read but no position taken. Distinct vocabulary from verdict / severity — the question is different: an assessment judges a verdict, not a record. PFS10-VC3.

target_review.description: Nested VerificationReview by value, via cross-file $ref to verification_review.json per PFS10-X2. The Python carries the VerificationReview as a value (frozen dataclass with no id); the schema expresses it via cross-file $ref for same-namespace resolution. Nested-by-value vs. reference-by-id choice banked in PFS10 OQ4. PFS10-VC4.

comment.description: Required prose explaining the assessment. Empty string admitted (matches StructuralAdvisory.comment posture). PFS10-VC2.

suggested_signature.description: Optional prose the commenter thinks the check might add (e.g., 'also check told_by listener, not just literal participant'). Free-form, intended as inspiration for the verifier maintainer — not actionable code. Python default None → omitted when None.
